steps.title: Plant the verticals first
steps.description: Draw two firm vertical lines for the outer edges of the door frame — make them truly vertical, using the edge of your paper as a guide if needed. Verticals are the skeleton of any architectural sketch; if they lean, the whole building will look unstable.

steps.title: Lay in the horizontals with a gentle converge
steps.description: Add the horizontal lines: the top of the door frame, the step at the bottom, and the sign band above the door. These lines converge very gently toward a distant vanishing point — just a degree or two of angle. Do not force strict perspective; a natural, eyeballed slope is enough to suggest depth.

steps.title: Sketch the door rectangle and step
steps.description: Within the frame, draw the door itself — a tall rectangle that fills most of the opening, with a little frame gap on each side. Add the small step below as a simple flat box shape projecting forward from the threshold.

steps.title: Add door panels and hardware
steps.description: Divide the door surface with a few recessed panel rectangles — two or three stacked vertically is classic. Add a simple knob or bar handle on one side. These details make the door feel solid without requiring precise drafting.

steps.title: Sketch the sign and wall above
steps.description: Above the door frame, draw a horizontal sign rectangle. Leave the wall on either side mostly quiet — just a few subtle brick or plaster texture marks, not a full pattern. The contrast between busy (sign, door) and quiet (wall) is what gives the drawing breathing room.

steps.title: Add the potted plant or window
steps.description: On one side of the doorway, sketch either a potted plant — a simple pot with loose, gestural leaf shapes that spill upward — or a small window with a sill. Either element softens the geometry and makes the scene feel lived-in. Keep it sketchy; it should not compete with the door.

steps.title: Drop in shadow and ground the scene
steps.description: Decide where the light falls — one side of the door frame should be in shadow. Fill the recessed door panels and the underside of the sign with soft hatching. Add a shadow the step casts on the ground and a cast shadow from the door frame on the wall. A few ground strokes beneath the step anchor the whole scene.
